subject: Sociology
title: Racial Categories Change Over Time
description: A fast breakdown of Racial Categories Change Over Time for builders.

hook.text: The U.S. Census has changed its racial categories 26 times since 1790. If race were natural, why keep redefining it?

buildup.text: In 1890, the Census tracked mulatto, quadroon, and octoroon. By 1930, those vanished into a single Black category.

discovery.text: The one-drop rule defined anyone with any Black ancestry as Black. No other group had a similar rule applied.

twist.text: Hispanic wasn't a racial category until 1970. Today, millions check it while also identifying as white or Black.

climax.text: Racial categories reflect political needs, not natural divisions. They change when power dynamics shift.

punchline.text: If race were natural, the Census wouldn't need to reinvent it.

quiz.question: What does the changing U.S. Census reveal about race?
options: Racial categories reflect politics, not biology
Race has remained consistent throughout history
The Census has never tracked race
